steps.title: Find the per-orbit difference
steps.content:
The second moon takes $269$ days per orbit, and the first moon takes $236$ days per orbit:
$$269-236=33$$

steps.title: Multiply by the orbit count
steps.content:
For $31$ orbits, multiply the per-orbit difference by $31$:
$$31\cdot33=1023$$
The answer is 1023.
